_provenance: VENDORED COPY of ordinance/config/ship-essential-winhub.json (fleet repo, Admiral-maintained; merged fleet#60, seeded from coordination/runbooks/winhub-bootstrap.md 2026-07-21). Copied into tender 2026-07-21 for shipyard#2998. Tender consumes a vendored snapshot — consistent with how it vendors sunfish contracts (src/vendor/) and bundle/catalog resources — rather than a live fetch to the fleet repo (the Toolbox must work offline and from a signed .app that has no fleet-repo checkout). STALENESS: this is a point-in-time copy; when the ordinance source changes (a new reclaimable service, a reclassification), re-vendor this file. The Ships view surfaces the vendored source + copy date so an operator can see it may lag. This is the SINGLE guard: unknown = essential = no control (fail-safe below).
_classification_rule: A service is 'reclaimable' (controllable: Start/Stop) ONLY if its name matches a reclaimable pattern AND no essential pattern. Anything else — essential-matched OR unknown — is 'essential' (status-only, no stop affordance). Patterns are case-insensitive substrings matched against the Windows service name. Same fail-safe as the battle-stations telemetry probe (shipyard#2995).
